channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israeli strikes kill a family of 8 in Gaza and destroy a century-old market in Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike in Gaza killed a family of eight, including children, Palestinian medical officials said Sunday, as Israeli forces battled Hamas in the territory's north and destroyed a century-old market in southern Lebanon while pursuing Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In an engineering feat, mechanical SpaceX arms catch Starship rocket booster back at the launch pad
item.description: SpaceX pulled off the boldest test flight yet of its enormous Starship rocket on Sunday, catching the returning booster back at the launch pad with mechanical arms. A jubilant Elon Musk called it “science fiction without the fiction part.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Determination to rebuild follows Florida's hurricanes with acceptance that storms will come again
item.description: VENICE, Fla. (AP) — No sooner had residents of the Bahia Vista Gulf condominium complex dug out and from Hurricane Helene than they were faced with the same daunting cleanup from new damage inflicted by Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump tested the limits on using the military at home. If elected again, he plans to go further
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During his first term as president, Donald Trump tested the limits of how he could use the military to achieve policy goals. If given a second term, the Republican and his allies are preparing to go much further, reimagining the military as an all-powerful tool to deploy on U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What you should do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives — emails, social media and bank accounts, photos, chat messages and more — that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon are in the crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The U.N. peacekeeping mission in southern Lebanon said that Israeli tanks “forcibly entered” one of their positions early Sunday, as Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu demanded they leave the area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Carolina football player dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: UNC had honored Tylee Craft, 23, during Saturday's game. Coach Mack Brown, however, said he hadn't learned of Craft's death until after the game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden views Hurricane Milton damage in Florida
item.description: President Joe Biden on Sunday will survey the devastation inflicted on Florida's Gulf Coast by Hurricane Milton as he urges Congress to approve additional emergency disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Prison operator under federal scrutiny spent millions settling Tennessee mistreatment claims
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The leading private prison company in the U.S. has spent more than $4.4 million to settle dozens of complaints alleging mistreatment — including at least 22 inmate deaths — at its Tennessee prisons and jails since 2016.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, TRAVIS LOLLER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the states we could be waiting on to declare a Harris-Trump winner
item.description: Voters are increasingly likely to have to wait past Election Day to find out who will become their next president. Among the battleground states, Georgia could be the first to be called, even as early as midnight, some experts predict.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Your Morning: Biden to visit Florida, 993k still without power
item.description: Welcome to the Your Morning newsletter featuring the latest news across America! It’s Oct. 13, 2024, and there’s a lot happening today.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Arkansas father arrested after allegedly killing man he found with his missing 14-year-old daughter
item.description: An Arkansas father was arrested after he allegedly shot and killed a man who he found in a vehicle with his missing 14-year-old daughter, according to the sheriff's office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arizona man found dead in Starbucks bathroom after allegedly shooting himself
item.description: A man shot and killed himself in a Starbucks bathroom in Gilbert, Arizona, after he acted erratically and pointed a gun at another person, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iowa mom's boyfriend nearly got away with murder — until a zipper changed everything
item.description: Dr. Michael Baden recounts how a nondescript zipper imprint helped prosecutors put Ivan "Sam" Brammer behind bars in the death of his girlfriend, Ilene Gowan, in a new Fox Nation show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina’s mountains, the roar of gas-powered generators is both unpleasant and essential.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Aoun Angueira, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Floridians cleaning up from Hurricane Milton are hampered by a widespread fuel shortage
item.description: CORTEZ, Fla. (AP) — Floridians recovering from Hurricane Milton, many of whom were journeying home after fleeing hundreds of miles to escape the storm, spent much of Saturday searching for gas as a fuel shortage gripped the state. In St.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY ONEIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ lawsuit filed against Virginia for voter list purge
item.description: With Election Day just around the corner, the Commonwealth is in hot water after the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced it is suing the state for illegally striking voters off of voter lists.
item.dc:creator: Allison Williams, NewsNation

item.title: Puerto Rico 'most wanted' homicide fugitive arrested in North Carolina
item.description: A "highly dangerous" fugitive accused of murder in Puerto Rico has been arrested in Halifax County, according to multiple agencies.
item.dc:creator: Starletta Watson, NewsNation

item.title: Colorado family trying to get dad home after falling ill on European cruise
item.description: A Colorado family is desperately trying to get their dad from Englewood home after a sudden and severe illness in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Kerridge, NewsNation

item.title: Harris helps pack aid boxes for North Carolina hurricane victims ahead of Sunday campaign push
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris on Saturday helped pack diapers into boxes of personal care products destined for North Carolina hurricane victims, agreeing with one helper who said “it takes a village.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UCLA professor says he’s homeless due to low pay
item.description: (KTLA) -- In a now-viral TikTok video, Dr. Daniel McKeown, a professor at UCLA, claims low pay has left him homeless, shocking many of his followers. "Hi everyone, my name is Daniel, and I’m an astrophysics professor at UCLA.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Whooping cough cases surge to record levels in US, CDC says
item.description: Whooping cough cases have increased to record levels since 2014, with four times as many cases compared to last year, data from the CDC showed. There have been more than 16,000 cases this year alone and two confirmed deaths, statistics revealed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: Dad gets dragged by car operated by 9-year-old son
item.description: A Toledo man was injured Sunday after he allegedly took his 9-year-old son on a driving lesson while drunk.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What's behind the northern lights that dazzled the sky farther south than normal
item.description: Another in a series of unusually strong solar storms hitting Earth produced stunning skies full of pinks, purples, greens and blues farther south than normal, including into parts of Germany, the United Kingdom, New England and New York City.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, clear debris after Hurricane Milton tore through Florida
item.description: LITHIA, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS O'MEARA, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 Europeans engineered $95M Texas Lotto win with over 25M tickets
item.description: It was successful for one group last year, but Texas Lottery officials have made changes to inhibit anyone else from trying it.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Kentucky woman arrested after dismembered body parts found 'cooked' inside oven
item.description: A witness told state police that she was reportedly "casting spells" and being "confrontational" the day prior to the discovery.
item.dc:creator: Jace O'Barto, Fox 5

item.title: Montana businessman gets 2 years in prison for role in Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the US Capitol
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A Montana business owner and supporter of former President Donald Trump has been sentenced to two years in federal prison for his role in the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol that interrupted certification of the 2020 Electoral College vote.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida deputies help pregnant woman in labor during Milton
item.description: Polk County Sheriff Grady Judd said that some of the office's deputies helped transport a pregnant woman who was going into labor to the hospital early Thursday morning, as Hurricane Milton brought widespread power outages, high winds and flooding to Central Florida.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Satellite images show before and after Hurricane Milton along Gulf Coast
item.description: Satellite images showcases the widespread damage and destruction left behind in Florida after Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, KRON4

item.title: A New York village known for its majestic mute swans faces a difficult choice after one is killed
item.description: MANLIUS, N.Y. (AP) — Elegant white swans have an outsize presence in this upstate New York village measuring less than 2 square miles. Their likeness is on village flags, community centers and welcome signs. “Swan Fest” is celebrated each fall.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taylor Swift donates $5 million to Hurricane Helene and Milton relief efforts
item.description: “This contribution will help communities rebuild and recover, providing essential food, clean water, and supplies to people affected by these devastating storms," Feeding America CEO Claire Babineaux-Fontenot said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: NASA video shows Hurricane Milton from space
item.description: Video taken from the International Space Station puts the massive size of Hurricane Milton into perspective.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: In remote mountain communities cut off by Helene, residents look to the skies for aid
item.description: RAMSEYTOWN, N.C. (AP) — As the Black Hawk helicopter slowly descends in Ramseytown, North Carolina, a plume of sand kicks up. When the dust settles, the sprawling sea of stones and twisted metal beams becomes clear.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tropical dictionary: Words to know as active hurricane season continues
item.description: Let’s dive into some of the frequently used words and phrases that are on repeat during this busy week of hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Alex Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Hurricane Milton slams Florida as a Category 3 storm; leaving millions in the dark
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
